title: New York blocks large data centers for a year  | AP News
excerpt: New York is blocking new large data centers that fuel artificial intelligence for up to a year to protect the environment and energy grid. Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order Tuesday for the country's first statewide moratorium on hyperscale data centers. These facilities require massive amounts of energy and water to cool thousands of servers. The governor's order pauses state permitting and directs regulators to create standards addressing environmental impacts and energy demand. Tech companies argue this could hurt job growth and cede ground to China in AI development. The decision also carries political significance for Hochul's reelection campaign and the state's congressional races.
content_html:
NEW YORK (AP) — No large data centers can be built in New York for up to a year as the state creates rules to protect the environment and its energy grid from the power-hungry facilities fueling artificial intelligence.

Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order Tuesday imposing the country’s first statewide moratorium on hyperscale data centers, which house thousands of computer servers and require massive amounts of energy and a steady supply of water to keep cool.

The move pushes the state into a raging debate over how to regulate the AI industry, as concerns over rising electric bills and environmental risks collide with a desire to stimulate local economies and foster the U.S. tech sector.

“The bottom line is that progress shouldn’t arrive with a higher utility bill, deleted water supply or noise pollution, so we have no choice but to address these challenges created by these massive facilities,” Hochul said at a celebratory signing ceremony in Brooklyn.

President Donald Trump has warned states not to slap regulations on the AI industry, echoing tech companies in arguing such moves hamper job growth and cede ground to China in a race to lead in the rapidly growing field.

Earlier this year, Maine seemed poised to establish a similar moratorium. But the measure was vetoed by Democratic Gov. Janet Mills because it would have blocked a proposed data center in a town that has struggled after a mill closed.

Moratoriums have been proposed in at least a dozen states but have not gotten far, though some counties and municipalities have imposed their own temporary bans.

New York’s executive order pauses state permitting for new large data centers and directs state regulators to create standards that address environmental impacts, energy demand, water usage and other factors, the governor’s office said.

Dan Diorio of the Data Center Coalition, a trade association, said the moratorium “will ensure that those investments, jobs, and economic activity flow elsewhere rather than to New York — with impacts far beyond the data center industry.”

The decision in New York also carries political significance for Hochul’s reelection campaign and the state’s tight congressional races this fall, as Democrats move to address affordability concerns over high utility bills. In addition, the governor this year softened New York’s ambitious goals to reduce greenhouse gases, citing rising energy costs for consumers.

Hochul’s Republican opponent in the governor’s race, Nassau County Executive Bruce Blakeman, opposes a statewide moratorium and says local governments should be allowed to strike deals with tech companies for data center projects that promise enough economic benefits.

In a statement Tuesday, Blakeman said the governor “doesn’t work with local governments and business leaders to figure out how to get things done,” while pledging to “modernize our economy to bring costs down and create good jobs.”

The state Legislature this year approved its own moratorium bill, but Hochul’s office described the legislation as complex and said it needed additional work. Instead, the governor opted for an executive order that would take effect immediately.

State Sen. Kristen Gonzalez, a Democrat who sponsored the legislation, joined the governor during the signing ceremony. “If Big Tech is coming onto our turf, it should be on our terms,” she said.

New York, at this stage, has not been a destination for the largest hyperscale data centers.

sentences.zh: 霍楚尔在州长竞选中的共和党对手、拿骚县行政长官布鲁斯·布莱克曼(Bruce Blakeman)反对全州范围内的暂停，并表示应该允许地方政府与科技公司就承诺足够经济效益的数据中心项目达成协议。

blocks.html_en: In a statement Tuesday, Blakeman said the governor “doesn’t work with local governments and business leaders to figure out how to get things done,” while pledging to “modernize our economy to bring costs down and create good jobs.”

sentences.en: In a statement Tuesday, Blakeman said the governor “doesn’t work with local governments and business leaders to figure out how to get things done,” while pledging to “modernize our economy to bring costs down and create good jobs.”
sentences.zh: 布莱克曼在周二的一份声明中表示，州长“没有与地方政府和商界领袖合作来找出如何把事情做好”，同时承诺“实现经济现代化，以降低成本并创造良好的就业机会”。

blocks.html_en: The state Legislature this year approved its own moratorium bill, but Hochul’s office described the legislation as complex and said it needed additional work. Instead, the governor opted for an executive order that would take effect immediately.

sentences.en: The state Legislature this year approved its own moratorium bill, but Hochul’s office described the legislation as complex and said it needed additional work.

sentences.en: Instead, the governor opted for an executive order that would take effect immediately.
sentences.zh: 相反，州长选择了一项立即生效的行政命令。

sentences.zh: 州参议员。

sentences.zh: 该法案的发起人、民主党人克里斯汀·冈萨雷斯 (Kristen Gonzalez) 也参加了州长的签字仪式。

sentences.en: “If Big Tech is coming onto our turf, it should be on our terms,” she said.
sentences.zh: “如果大型科技公司进入我们的地盘，就应该按照我们的条件行事，”她说。

blocks.html_en: New York, at this stage, has not been a destination for the largest hyperscale data centers.

content_html_zh:
更新的 [小时]:[分钟] [AMPM] [时区], [完整月] [日], [年]

纽约（美联社）——纽约州在一年内无法建造大型数据中心，因为纽约州制定了保护环境及其能源网络免受人工智能高耗电设施影响的规定。

政府。 凯西·霍赫尔 (Kathy Hochul) 周二签署了一项行政命令，首次在全州范围内暂停超大规模数据中心的建设，这些数据中心容纳了数千台计算机服务器，需要大量能源和稳定的水供应来保持冷却。

由于对电费上涨和环境风险的担忧与刺激当地经济和培育美国科技行业的愿望发生冲突，此举促使该州就如何监管人工智能行业展开激烈辩论。

霍赫尔在布鲁克林举行的庆祝签字仪式上表示：“最重要的是，进步不应伴随着更高的公用事业费用、取消供水或噪音污染，因此我们别无选择，只能应对这些大型设施带来的挑战。”

唐纳德·特朗普总统警告各州不要对人工智能行业实施监管，他与科技公司一样认为，此类举措会阻碍就业增长，并在快速增长的领域的领先竞争中让位于中国。

今年早些时候，缅因州似乎准备实行类似的暂停令。 但该措施遭到民主党州长否决。 珍妮特·米尔斯 (Janet Mills) 的理由是，它会阻止在一个工厂关闭后陷入困境的城镇中拟建的数据中心。

至少有十几个州已提议暂停实施，但尚未取得进展，尽管一些县和市已经实施了自己的临时禁令。

州长办公室表示，纽约州的行政命令暂停了州政府对新建大型数据中心的许可，并指示州监管机构制定解决环境影响、能源需求、用水和其他因素的标准。

行业协会数据中心联盟的丹·迪奥里奥表示，暂停“将确保这些投资、就业和经济活动流向其他地方而不是纽约——其影响远远超出了数据中心行业。”

随着民主党着手解决对高额公用事业账单的负担能力担忧，纽约的这一决定对于霍赫尔的连任竞选和该州今年秋天激烈的国会竞选也具有政治意义。 此外，州长今年以消费者能源成本上升为由，软化了纽约减少温室气体排放的雄心勃勃的目标。

霍楚尔在州长竞选中的共和党对手、拿骚县行政长官布鲁斯·布莱克曼(Bruce Blakeman)反对全州范围内的暂停，并表示应该允许地方政府与科技公司就承诺足够经济效益的数据中心项目达成协议。

布莱克曼在周二的一份声明中表示，州长“没有与地方政府和商界领袖合作来找出如何把事情做好”，同时承诺“实现经济现代化，以降低成本并创造良好的就业机会”。

州立法机关今年批准了自己的暂停法案，但霍楚尔办公室称该立法非常复杂，并表示需要开展更多工作。 相反，州长选择了一项立即生效的行政命令。

州参议员。 该法案的发起人、民主党人克里斯汀·冈萨雷斯 (Kristen Gonzalez) 也参加了州长的签字仪式。 “如果大型科技公司进入我们的地盘，就应该按照我们的条件行事，”她说。

现阶段纽约还不是最大的超大规模数据中心的目的地。
